microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program from a 2-person operation into a **platformized system**, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led 0→1 development of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that **reduced operational toil by 30%** and transitioned execution to a scalable, repeatable delivery model.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a **rack-level drill program in 4 months** with a 94% recovery rate — surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a continuous validation framework across distributed infrastructure.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract, coordinating technical and stakeholder communications across multiple divisions.

Scaled team leverage through AI-driven automation, building an internal AI agent and restructuring planning workflows to **reduce planning cycle time by 39%** and increase drill capacity by 21% without adding headcount.
microsoft_2023: Championed adoption of AI-driven code generation workflows, conducting user demos and training sessions that drove utilization across **14 key engineering teams** and saved 37 engineering hours monthly by reducing manual development toil.

Shaped the product roadmap to include **intent-based YAML generation** by facilitating 11+ structured user interviews with Azure service teams, surfacing critical feature gaps and translating stakeholder needs into actionable technical requirements.

Improved data integrity and cross-team retrieval by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search tool**, cutting average lookup time by 83% and reducing friction in engineering workflows.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched cloud regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework, coordinating execution across 140+ engineering and operations teams.

Drove a product strategy to eliminate manual toil during region launches, conducting discovery with 20+ service teams to surface **81 hours of per-region manual effort** and building a phased roadmap to automate the highest-impact critical paths.

Enabled leadership to identify automation gaps and allocate resources efficiently by engineering a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil trends, resource utilization, and bottlenecks across 140+ teams in real time.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping hardware and software dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and coordinating releases across cross-functional engineering teams.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, managing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilize the developer intake process for a robotics software platform under active production deployment.

Drove cross-functional alignment between IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and reducing integration risk across a large-scale embedded systems environment.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by optimizing end-to-end operations and managing the full scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals through a redesigned CRM process that improved stakeholder communication and delivery tracking.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and boosted conversion rates by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, including website optimization and Google Business profile enhancements to improve lead quality.

Improved leadership pricing decisions by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ historical project records, identifying systemic margin leaks and delivering data-driven recommendations that were adopted into the company's pricing model.

tailoring_notes: 'Release Management' from the JD woven into amazon_robotics bullet#1 and microsoft_ft bullet#1 — framing drills and OS migrations as coordinated hardware/software release events with defined quality gates.
'Resource utilization' and 'bottlenecks' from the JD's Resource Allocation section woven into microsoft_2022 bullet#3 — Power BI dashboard explicitly framed around those JD terms.

'Cross-functional collaboration' and 'stakeholder communication' from the JD woven into microsoft_ft bullet#4 — sovereign-cloud execution described as multi-division bridge lead with internal/external comms ownership.

'Risk management' and 'mitigation' from the JD woven into amazon_robotics bullet#1 — dependency mapping and zero-downtime outcome directly mirror JD language around identifying and minimizing delivery risk.

'Documentation and process improvement' from the JD woven into microsoft_2023 bullet#3 — metadata standards and semantic search migration framed as documentation quality and retrieval efficiency improvements.
